{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I perform sentence-level copyediting only (clarity, concision, tone) or also make content-level changes such as reframing claims and checking/adjusting logical consistency and evidence alignment?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request to 'Clean this up' most directly indicates sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and readability.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve and, if needed, clarify any uncertainty qualifiers so as not to overstate what the results establish.",
    "RX.2": "While polishing, avoid amplifying claims beyond what the text supports (e.g., unwarranted guarantees).",
    "RX.3": "Keep the work within the scope of stylistic cleanup unless the user authorizes deeper content edits.",
    "RX.4": "Ensure wording does not introduce misleading guidance that could cause misuse of results or misinterpretation in operational contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear references (e.g., to Table 1 and specific methods) so readers can trace claims to their sources."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I perform sentence-level copyediting only (clarity, concision, tone) or also make content-level changes such as reframing claims and checking/adjusting logical consistency and evidence alignment?

rationale.R1.6: The request to 'Clean this up' most directly indicates sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and readability.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve and, if needed, clarify any uncertainty qualifiers so as not to overstate what the results establish.
rationale.RX.2: While polishing, avoid amplifying claims beyond what the text supports (e.g., unwarranted guarantees).
rationale.RX.3: Keep the work within the scope of stylistic cleanup unless the user authorizes deeper content edits.
rationale.RX.4: Ensure wording does not introduce misleading guidance that could cause misuse of results or misinterpretation in operational contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear references (e.g., to Table 1 and specific methods) so readers can trace claims to their sources.
